title: Almond Flour Banana Blueberry Muffins
instructions: Preheat oven to 350F.
In a large bowl, mix together almond flour, cinnamon and baking soda (and salt if using)
In a separate bowl, mix together the mashed bananas, eggs, and yogurt
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until well combined (do not over mix). Gently fold in the blueberries.
Add to 8x8 pan and bake for about 40 minutes, or until an inserted fork comes out clean.

title: Avocado Toast with Egg
instructions: Toast the bread (make sure it is toasted enough so it is hard but not burnt, this will make it taste buttery)
With olive oil, fry the egg hard side up
Mash avocado
Assemble: put avocado on toast, then Everything but the Bagel seasoning, then egg on top

title: Banana Apple Carrot Bread
ingredients: cup mashed very ripe banana (about 2 small bananas)
Tbsp avocado oil or 4 Tbsp butter
instructions: These are deliberately very moist (and moister than regular muffins) to ensure they are easy for baby to chew and swallow, so expect that from the interior.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F
Place the banana, apple, carrot, milk, butter or oil, and egg into a medium bowl. Stir together.
Stir in a separate bowl the flour, oats, cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
Gently stir in the dry mixture into the wet mixture. Then put into an 8x8 baking dish.
Bake for about 20 minutes, until a fork inserted into the center comes out pretty much cleanly.
Cut into small cubes and flash freeze.

title: Banana Bread
ingredients: eggs/substitutes (3 Tbsp ground flaxseed and 8 Tbsp water stirred and let sit for 5 minutes before using)
cup almond flour and 1 cup chickpea flour
cup or so chocolate chip or walnut mix-ins
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees
In large bowl, beat oil and honey together with a whisk. Add the eggs and beat well, then whisk in the mashed bananas and milk.
Add the baking soda, salt, and cinnamon and whisk to blend. Switch to a big spoon and stir in the flour, just until combined. Gently fold in mix-ins.
Pour into 8x8 inch pan, for about 40 minutes or so, until cooked through.

title: Banana Pancakes
ingredients: 1/2 medium ripe banana (best with brown spots)
/3 cup flour (mix of almond and coconut flour I like)
instructions: In a medium bowl, mash your banana. It’s really best if your banana is nice and ripe with brown spots on the peel!

Crack two eggs into the bowl with the mashed banana and whisk with a fork until well combined. It’s okay if there are lumps of banana. Next, add in flour and milk and mix to combine. Batter should be similar to traditional pancake batter.

Lightly coat a nonstick pan or nonstick griddle with butter, coconut oil, vegan butter or olive oil and place over medium heat. Once the pan is hot, add 1 heaping tablespoon of the batter to the griddle for each pancake and cook for 2-3 minutes until pancakes slightly puff up, are golden underneath and you see a few bubbles along the edges.

Flip cakes and cook until golden brown on the underside. If you find that pancakes are browning too quickly then you need to lower the heat. I normally start on medium heat, then decrease to medium low later so that my pancakes don't burn. If at any point your griddle starts smoking or if your pancakes are burning, it means your pan is too hot.
Repeat with more oil/butter and remaining batter.

title: Beet Mushroom Risotto
ingredients: ½ cups liquid (up to ¼ cup white wine, water, chicken broth)
instructions: Saute onion with olive oil in instant pot for a few minutes until soft.
Add rice to pot, stir in any white wine and cook for a few minutes. Add rest of water/chicken broth.
Add frozen beets and mushrooms to instant pot and stir to combine.
Set instant pot to manual for 35 minutes.
When done, do quick release. Sir in grated parmesan cheese.

title: Black Bean Brownies
ingredients: can black beans, rinsed and drained
cup chocolate chips, half mini chips, half regular size
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees
Combine all ingredients except chocolate chips into a food processor and blend extremely well, at least 5 minutes.
Stir in chocolate chips and put into 8x8 inch pan. Bake for 15-18 minutes.

title: Black lentils
ingredients: container prepared trader Joe's black lentils
½ container Trader Joe's red pepper with eggplant and garlic spread ½ container
Optional tumeric, ginger, garlic or onion powder to taste
instructions: Mix together.

title: Blended seasonings
instructions: ¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
Chili Powder Seasoning:
⅛ cup sweet paprika
½ tsp smoked paprika
½ tsp garlic powder
½ tsp cayenne pepper
½ tsp onion powder
tsp dried oregano
tsp ground cumin
Mac and Cheese Seasoning:
Equal parts mustard powder and garlic powder
½ parts onion powder and Italian seasoning
¼ part ground black pepper

title: Bolognese Sauce
ingredients: Or a pack of frozen mushrooms
instructions: Sautee ground meat
Combine everything else except pasta in instant pot, set to 7 minutes on high. Slow release.
Instant Pot from Frozen:

Combine 8 oz pasta (tested with spiral quinoa and brown rice pasta from Trader Joe’s), 1 ¾ cup water, and ½ of the thawed turkey bolognese (thaw from frozen for at least 24 hours). Cook on manual for 2 minutes, quick release. Stir to combine juices and let sit for a few minutes to soak up the liquid.

title: Breakfast Quesadilla
ingredients: Add-ins (chicken, black beans, avocado, veggies (cauliflower, onion, peppers, tomatoes))
instructions: Heat olive oil in skillet. Add wrap and top with cheese and any add-ins. Lightly top with these ingredients if using only one wrap and folding in half. Heavily top if adding another wrap on top and then heating on other side.

title: Brown Rice
ingredients: cups brown rice (any kind except quick kind)
instructions: ½ cups water or broth
Directions: Manual 15 minutes, slow release

title: Burrito Casserole
ingredients: packages fresh spinach or other greens
½ cups shredded taco-cheese blend (or mozzarella if not available)
instructions: With olive oil, saute ground turkey and then spinach. Remove and keep on the side.
In a large bowl, combine rice, salsa and oregano.
In another bowl, combine refried beans, corn, cumin, and chili powder.

In a large baking dish (12 x 8 inch), spread ½ of the rice mixture. Layer on all of the refried bean mixture and then half of the cheese. Layer on the ground turkey and sauteed spinach. Then top with the remaining rice mixture, then remaining cheese.
Bake at 350 degrees for 35 minutes. Uncovered. Optional - sprinkle on cilantro.

title: Butternut Squash Risotto
ingredients: container cut butternut squash (about a pound or so)
½ cups liquid (up to ¼ cup white wine, water, chicken broth)
instructions: Saute chopped leeks with olive oil in instant pot for a few minutes until soft.
Add rice to pot, stir in any white wine and cook for a few minutes. Add rest of water/chicken broth.
Add cut butternut squash.
Set instant pot to manual for 35 minutes.
When done, do quick release. Sir in fresh sage, black pepper, and grated parmesan cheese. Stir in fresh baby spinach.

title: Butternut Squash Tortellini
ingredients: container cut butternut squash (1 ½-2 lbs)
instructions: Add everything to Instant pot except spinach and parmesan cheese
Set instant pot to manual for 5 minutes.
When done, do quick release after a couple minutes. Sir in spinach until wilted. Then stir in ½ cup parmesan cheese. Serve with extra parmesan cheese on top.

title: Carrot fritters
instructions: Whisk together. Clump into about 7-8 patties (won't stick together before baking).
Bake at 375 degrees for 10 minutes, then flip to other side and bake for another 10 minutes. Take out when mostly browned but not burnt.

title: Cashew coconut curry
ingredients: Cilantro (fresh or dried) - about 1 Tbsp dried or a small bunch fresh
instructions: Add all the ingredients except frozen chicken, cilantro (if fresh) (include in this step if dried) and coconut milk into the instant pot.
Stir, and then add frozen chicken on top.
Set instant pot to manual at 15 minutes on high. Natural release.
Strir in cilantro if fresh, and coconut milk.

title: Cashew cream parfait
ingredients: Any toppings (fruit, chopped nut, coconut, chia seeds, flaxseed, hemp seeds)
instructions: Combine all ingredients except the add-ons. Blend together and refrigerate for at least a few hours.
Add in toppings and enjoy.
Can keep in the fridge for a week or so

title: Chana Masala
instructions: Add all ingredients to instant pot. Cook on manual for 5 minutes.
Quick release once done.

title: ChatGPT recipe ideas
ingredients: cups frozen mixed veggies (broccoli, carrots, cauliflower)
cups frozen veggie mix (carrots, peppers, zucchini)
instructions: Dump everything into Instant Pot
Quick release + stir
Why it works
Lentils cook fast → super creamy
Veggies = ~50% of volume
Mild flavor = kid friendly
🌮 2. Mexican Black Bean Veggie Bowl
Protein: ~20–25g per serving
Dump + mix
Quick release
Greek yogurt (adds protein 🔥)
Avocado
Tortillas or rice (optional)
🍛 3. Chickpea Coconut Veggie Curry
Protein: ~20g per serving
Dump everything
Stir + optional squeeze of lime
Boost protein
Add 1/2 cup red lentils → pushes protein higher
🍲 4. High-Protein Minestrone (No Pasta Needed)
Protein: ~22g per serving
Dump + stir
Optional
Add small pasta after cooking (for kids)
🥦 5. Lentil & Veggie Sloppy Joe Filling (SO GOOD)
Protein: ~23g per serving
Dump everything
Natural release 5–10 min
Serve
On buns, wraps, or over rice
Kids LOVE this one
🔥 Pro Tips (this is the real game-changer)
Keep it 50% veggies easily
Just follow this rule:👉 “At least 2 cups frozen veggies per 1 cup beans/lentils”
Always hit protein targets
Add one of these if needed:
Greek yogurt (serve on top)
Extra lentils
Hemp seeds (blend in sauces)
Batch once, eat 2–3 days
All of these:
Store great
Freeze well
Taste better next day
If you want, I can:
Turn this into a weekly meal prep plan
Or customize for your kids (like “less spice / hidden veggies / finger food versions”)

title: Cheesy Chicken Fajita Soup
ingredients: ~2 cups frozen or fresh corn (prefer frozen roasted Trader Joe’s corn)
pack frozen roasted bell pepper and onion from Trader Joe’s
instructions: Add everything to the instant pot except chicken, yogurt, and cheese.
Then, layer the chicken breast on top (don’t add more than 1 layer of chicken)
If using frozen chicken, set the instant pot to 12 minutes manual. If fresh, 10 minutes manual. Slow release.

Immediately, so still hot, stir in greek yogurt first. Then stir in cheddar cheese. Then stir in baby spinach (if soup is not hot when you stir in the spinach, then saute spinach so wilts before stirring it in).

title: Chia and Flaxseed pudding
ingredients: Any toppings (fruit, chopped nuts or nut butter, shredded coconut)
instructions: Combine all ingredients except the add-ons. Cover and place in fridge overnight (for at least 5 hours).
Add in toppings and enjoy.
Can keep in the fridge for a week or so

title: Chia seed jam
instructions: Saute frozen berries in a sauce pan for about 10 minutes, with frequent stirring so doesn't stick to bottom of pan.
Mash the berries until desired texture (I do a rough mash with a potato masher so still some chunks)
Remove from heat and stir in chia seeds right away (while still very hot). As it cools, it will thicken.
Can keep in the fridge for a week or so

title: Chicken
instructions: Add one cup of liquid chicken bone broth. Add chicken breast.
Frozen: Manual 15 minutes, quick release
Fresh: Manual 9-10 minutes, slow release

title: Chicken bone broth
instructions: Add any cooked chicken bones to instant pot (I either cook a whole chicken first or use a store bought rotisserie chicken). Everything inedible on the chicken can go into the instant pot (including skin). I use the scraps from 2 store bought rotisserie chickens
Water to match top of chicken or as much as a little below the max fill line.
Directions: Manual 240 minutes, slow release

I freeze in ½ cup cubes that I then transfer to a ziplock bag to store. When using in place of liquid in the instant pot, microwave first so the recipe doesn't burn since it doesn't turn to liquid quickly enough in instant pot recipes to replace all liquid in frozen form.

title: Chicken Burrito Filling
instructions: Add all ingredients to instant pot. Cook on manual for 10 minutes.
Quick release once done.

title: Chicken Cacciatorie
ingredients: medium carrot, peeled and sliced into ½-inch rounds (or however you want)
medium red or green bell pepper, diced (about 1 cup)
⅓ cup halved kalamata olives (dont have to put)
instructions: Select the “Saute” setting on the Instant Pot. Once hot, add oil and chicken. Sear the chicken for 1-2 minutes per side.
Cancel the “Saute” setting. Add carrot, bell pepper, tomatoes, and oregano. Place lid on top of Instant Pot and secure. Set vent to “Sealing.”

Select the “Meat” setting on the Instant Pot. Adjust the time to 20 minutes on “High Pressure” and cook. After cooking, let the pressure naturally release for 15 minutes. Carefully switch the vent to “Venting” to release any remaining pressure. Remove lid.
Stir in capers and kalamata olives. Adjust flavor with salt and pepper.

title: Chicken meatballs
instructions: Mix all ingredients aside from the ground chicken together. Then incorporate into the ground chicken. Form small meatballs (about 2 Tbsp for each meatball) and put on a baking tray.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or so - until cooked through.

title: Chicken sausage farro stew
instructions: Add everything to the instant pot. Set to 7 minutes, slow release.
If using spinach instead of kale, stir in at the end while stew is still hot instead of before cooking.

title: Chicken Taco Soup
ingredients: (12 fluid ounces) can corn drained, or frozen corn
(19 fluid ounces) can black beans drained & rinsed
(13.5 fluid ounce) cans fire-roasted diced tomatoes with juices
instructions: Sauté the olive oil and onion in your Instant Pot for a few minutes. Cancel sauté.
Add the other soup ingredients, except the spinach and cheese, to your pressure cooker and give it a stir.
Ensure the lid is on "sealing". Cook on high pressure for 9 minutes if refrigerated chicken, 11 minutes if frozen chicken. It'll take about 10-15 minutes for it to get up to pressure.
Once the countdown has finished, do a quick pressure release.
Take the chicken out and shred it with two forks. Add it back into the soup.
Stir in the fresh baby spinach until wilted.
When ready to eat, top with shredded pepper jack cheese. Can also top with avocado.

title: Coconut Cauliflower Curry
instructions: Saute onion for 2 minutes. Add sweet potato and saute 3 minutes. Add cauliflower and a bit of saunt and saute another 5 minutes. Stir in 2 Tbsp curry powder, 1 Tbsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin, ¼ tsp cayenne. Add diced tomatoes and coconut milk. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat and simmer for about 10-20 minutes, until the cauliflower and sweet potatoes are tender.
Add chickpeas and spinach and cook until the spinach is wilted.

title: Coconut Chia Pudding with cantaloupe
ingredients: In a small bowl, whisk together coconut milk, water, chia seeds, and maple syrup until well mixed.

Cover and refrigerate until a gel-like mixture has formed, at least 2 hours. I recommend stirring the mixture after about an hour in the fridge to redistribute the chia seeds as they tend to settle to the bottom.
instructions: (imported — check formatting)

title: Cream of cauliflower soup
ingredients: ½ package of frozen diced onions
instructions: Add cauliflower, onions, chicken bone broth, and flour in a large pot. Heat, stirring occasionally until cauliflower is soft.
Remove from heat and add in milk, cheddar cheese, pepper, and Italian seasoning. Stir and then blend until mostly smooth.

title: Crispy roasted chickpeas
instructions: Crispy roasted chickpeas
Chickpeas
Spice mix (can make anything, for example garlic powder, onion powder, Italian seasoning, black pepper)
Directions: Add chickpeas to baking tray (don't overcrowd tray). Mix with spice blend, then drizzle with avocado oil and bake for about 45 minutes, until crispy.

Mac and cheese
Chili

title: Easy Spinach Tortillas
ingredients: ⅛ cups water (1-¼ cup depending on thickness desired)
instructions: Blend all ingredients together until fully blended. Will be a wet consistency.
Pour ⅓ cup in a saute pan. Sautee for about 2 minutes on one side, flip and then sautee for about 1 minute on the other side.
Can use a couple drops of oil if desired. But if you flip in time (don’t wait too long or will stick to pan), you don’t need oil.

title: Easy Veggie Chili
instructions: Add all ingredients to instant pot. Cook on manual for 10 minutes.
Quick release once done.

title: Farro
instructions: 2 ¼ cups pearled farro
2 ¾ cups water
7 minutes Manual, natural release

title: Farro corn bowl
instructions: Mix together and serve

title: Farro, Chicken, Veggies
ingredients: ½ cups liquid (so all ingredients aside from chicken are just covered)
⅓ bag frozen spinach or kale
instructions: Saute onion and carrots with oil in instant pot for a few minutes until soft.
Add rest of ingredients except chicken and spinach/kale. Stir together.
Put frozen chicken on top (don’t stir in).
Set instant pot to manual for 15 minutes.
Stir in frozen spinach/kale (while hot).

title: Feta orzo veggie bake
ingredients: About a tsp each of Italian seasoning, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper
Drizzle of avocado or olive oil
A couple containers of cut tomatoes
instructions: Add ingredients to a large baking dish in the order listed.
Bake for about 30-45 minutes.
Stirring a couple times to make sure all the orzo is fully coated in the broth and therefore fully cooked

title: Ginger Sweet Potato Coconut Milk Stew
ingredients: in piece fresh ginger, minced (prefer fresh, but powder okay too)
instructions: Add oil in dutch oven pan. Saute onions until soft, a few minutes. Add the chili flakes, coriander, cumin and turmeric. Saute for a minute, until very fragrant. Add ginger and garlic and cook for another minute.
Add sweet potatoes to the pot and stir to coat. Stir in the lentils. Add stock/water and stir, scraping up any brown bits at the bottom of the pot.
Bring to a boil. Then lower to a simmer with lid mostly covered. Simmer for about 30 minutes.
Then add coconut milk and greens. Simmer until greens are wilted.
Can add cilantro or lime wedges as garnish.

title: Grandma's chicken soup
ingredients: package chicken breast (at least a pound)
large carrots, chopped into large sticks (6-9 or so sticks per carrot)
Handful of fresh parsley, roughly chopped
sticks of celery, chopped a little smaller (optional)
instructions: Add everything to a large pot plus enough water or chicken bone broth as desired (I usually do a few inches above all the ingredients). Cover and simmer for 2 hours.
Once cooked, take out the chicken and shred and keep to the side. Blend the rest of the ingredients (can leave some veggie chunks as desired). Add back the shredded chicken and enjoy.

title: Granola
ingredients: ⅔ cup dried fruit (chopped if too big)
½ cup honey or maple syrup
instructions: Mix together oats, nuts/seeds, salt, cinnamon.
Mix in separate bowl melted coconut oil, honey or maple syrup and vanilla.

Stir both together very well. Then spread on a baking tray (keep together for clumps) and bake for 21 to 24 minutes (will not appear done but will crisp up as cooling). Add in coconut halfway through cooking. Cool for at least 45 minutes before breaking apart).

title: Greek Salad Dressing
ingredients: Tbsp fresh lemon juice, from one lemon
½ cup plain whole milk greek yogurt
⅓ cup extra version olive oil
¼ tsp dried thyme or other herbs
instructions: (imported — check formatting)

title: Green curry
cookTime: noodles based on back of bag (i do for 5 minutes). drain and keep aside.
ingredients: Wegmans frozen grilled veggies (has eggplant, peppers, onions) - I use ⅔ of a 54 oz family pack bag
container Thai kitchen green curry paste (4oz)
Brown rice noodles (I use half of an 8oz  bag, Wegmans brand)
Rotisserie chicken (taken apart) (I like Wegmans rosemary rotisserie chicken) (I used about ⅔ of a chicken)
instructions: Saute the veggies.
In a separate saucepan, heat up coconut milk and green curry paste just need to heat up enough to melt the green curry paste, about 3 minutes or so.
Combine all together - cooked noodles, veggies, chicken, sauce.

title: Green smoothie
ingredients: Almond milk, about a cup and a half (or until consistency desired)
instructions: Blend all together

title: High protein chicken salad
ingredients: Pickled or plain cut red onion
instructions: Combine everything and serve.

title: Indonesian Gado Gado
ingredients: ½ tsp fresh ginger or 1 tsp powdered ginger
¾ cup peanut butter (I like creamy Teddie All Natural)
⅓ cup low sodium soy sauce
instructions: Steam all items except bean sprouts, mung beans, and cucumbers. Note that you may want to steam them separately as some take longer than others to steam.

Whisk all peanut sauce ingredients together. You will likely need to continue adding a few Tbsp of water at a time until you get a liquidy consistency (but not too runny). Top steamed veggies with warmed peanut sauce.

title: Instant Pot Beans
description: RECIPEInstant Pot Dried Beans Pre-soaked:
ingredients: Add beans with  water filling up about 1-2  inch higher than the beans.
instructions: Chickpeas: Manual 5 minutes, natural release.
Black beans: Manual 7-8 minutes, natural release. (8 for softer)

title: Instant Pot Rolled Oats
instructions: Mix together in instant pot.
Manual for 4 minutes. Let sit for at least 20 minutes plugged in.
Stir in almond butter and eat with half a banana.

title: Italian Spaghetti Squash
ingredients: container grape tomatoes (about 10 oz), cut in half
About 1 cup white wine or chicken broth
instructions: Cut spaghetti squash in half lengthwise and scoop out seeds. Add 1 cup of water and metal rack to Instant Pot. Put the spaghetti squash on the metal rack.
Cook on manual high pressure for 7-8 minutes (depending on how firm want squash to be).
Once done, remove squash from skin with fork.
While squash is cooking, saute sliced mushrooms until tender. Add in grape tomatoes and continue cooking for another few minutes until soft.
Add white wine or chicken broth, and cook for another couple minutes until hot. Add fresh baby spinach and stir into mixture until wilted. Pour the mixture on top of the spaghetti squash.

title: Lemon Tahini Dressing
ingredients: to 4 tablespoons lemon juice, to taste
large clove garlic, pressed or minced
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
instructions: Can use lemons instead of lemon juice. Can use 1 whole lemon. Squeeze lemon juice out of lemon into bowl. Remove seeds that come out
Put in oil, garlic (can use garlic powder if dont have garlic), salt and pepper. Just stir with a spoon for about a minute.

title: Mac and cheese
description: RECIPEMac and Cheese Make Mac and cheese spice mixture:
instructions: Add as much pasta as you want to the pot, then fill up water to match the pasta, then stir in the spice mixture.
Set instant pot to 1 minute on high for any large pasta (such as penne) or 0 minutes on high or 1 minute on low for any small pasta (such as elbows)
I make the Mac and cheese spice mixture ahead of time and put in about 3 TBSP per box of pasta
After instant pot is done, stir in shredded cheddar cheese. If pasta is super dry after instant pot is done, can stir in some milk to soften before adding the cheddar cheese.+

title: Massaman Curry
ingredients: /2 lb. shrimp (seafood option — wild caught when possible)
large skinless chicken breast, cubed (meat option — free-range, local, organic when possible)
Tbsp coconut or avocado oil (if avoiding oil, sub water and add more as needed)
medium shallots, thinly sliced (or sub 1 small onion)
tsp whole cumin seed (or sub powder)
tsp whole coriander seed (or sub powder)
Tbsp red curry paste (we love Thai Kitchen brand)
½ cups baby potatoes cut into bite-size pieces
large carrots, peeled and diced 1/4-inch thick
14-oz. cans light fat coconut milk (sub up to 1 can with full-fat coconut milk for creamier, richer curry — texture shown in photos above)
dash each cardamom and nutmeg (if you don't have, omit)
Tbsp coconut aminos (or sub tamari or soy sauce, but start with less as they're saltier)
Tbsp maple syrup or coconut sugar (or sub stevia to taste)
Tbsp peanut butter (in place of traditional roasted peanuts — can sub roasted peanuts)
You have options for added protein or you can just stick with vegetables.
To keep it vegan-friendly, follow the link above to make Crispy Peanut Tofu.

Or, to keep this a 1-pot recipe, simply add (pressed) cubed extra-firm tofu to the curry in the last 10 minutes of cooking or sauté pressed tofu in a little oil and season with salt, pepper, and curry powder before cooking the curry. Set aside, then add back in in the last few minutes of cooking for best results.
Alternatively, add meat such as shrimp or chicken to the curry once it's simmering (during step 4) and simmer until cooked completely through.
Heat a large pot or dutch oven (we like this one) over medium heat. Once hot, add oil (or water) and shallot. Sauté 2 minutes, stirring frequently. Turn down heat if browning too quickly.
Add whole cumin and coriander seeds (or powder) and sauté for another 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently. Then add red curry paste and stir to combine. Cook for 1 minute more.

Add potatoes and carrots and stir to coat. Cook for 2 minutes. Then add coconut milk, water (starting with the lesser amount), cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, coconut aminos, maple syrup, and peanut butter. (Reserve lime juice for later).
The liquid should cover all of the ingredients — if it does not, add a bit more coconut milk or water to cover. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat.

Once it reaches a low boil, reduce heat to a simmer (add meat at this time if cooking with shrimp or chicken) and cook for 10-15 minutes uncovered. You don't want it boiling, so ensure it's cooking over low heat at a simmer.

Add lime in the last few minutes of cooking and stir. Then taste and adjust flavor as needed, adding more lime for acidity, salt or coconut aminos for saltiness, curry paste for heat / more intense curry taste, maple syrup for sweetness, cinnamon or nutmeg for warmth, or peanut butter for creaminess / more intense peanut flavor.
Stir and cook a few minutes more. Then turn off heat and let stand for at least 5 minutes before serving (this allows the flavors to meld).

To serve, divide between serving bowls and enjoy as is or with a side of rice, cauliflower rice, quinoa, or steamed greens (optional). Fresh lime juice, cilantro, and roasted peanuts (optional) make lovely additions as well.

title: Mediterranean Greek Chicken Bowls

Roasted veggies (tomatoes, red onion, mushrooms, broccoli, asparagus, etc.)
instructions: Put chicken and ½ - ¾ cup water in the instant pot. Put the combined seasonings on top.
Ensure the lid is on "sealing". Cook on high pressure for 9 minutes if refrigerated chicken, 11-12 minutes if frozen chicken. It'll take about 10-15 minutes for it to get up to pressure..
Take the chicken out and shred it with two forks. Keep any leftovers in the broth from the Instant Pot.

title: Oatmeal Coconut White Chocolate Cookies
ingredients: ½ cup smooth unsalted peanut or almond butter
⅓ cup maple syrup or honey
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees
In large bowl, whisk together oats, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and coconut
In another bowl, mash bananas and then whisk in the applesauce, nut butter, and maple syrup, until smooth.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and mix until just combined. Fold in the white chocolate chips.
Scoop into cookies, can flatten a bit before cooking because they will hardly spread during baking.
Bake for 15-18 minutes, until edges are golden brown. Don’t check bottom until after cooled, as cookies will break apart. Take out and let cool completely.

title: Overnight oats
ingredients: Any add-ins, as desired (can make homemade jam)
instructions: Mix together oats, milk, and yogurt. Let sit at least for 2 hours, at most 3 days. Mix in add-ins when ready to eat. Can serve cold or warm.

title: Palak Paneer
ingredients: package paneer (from Wegmans, otherwise need to steam the paneer first to soften a bit) or about 12 oz feta cheese block, chopped while instant pot is cooking
instructions: Add everything to Instant pot except paneer
Set instant pot to manual for 0 minutes on high or 1 minute on low.
Manual release.
Right away while still hot, blend everything until smooth and then add in chopped paneer or feta. Let sit for 15 minutes to soften the paneer.

title: Peanut butter oat bites
instructions: Microwave honey and peanut butter in a bowl. Whisk together.
Add all other ingredients and stir to combine. Form into balls. Refrigerate for at least 15 minutes before eating.

title: Persian Herbed Chickpea Stew
ingredients: cans chickpeas (or 1 cup dried chickpeas, cooked)
½ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
½ cup fresh cilantro, roughly chopped
½ cup fresh parsley, roughly chopped
¼ cup fresh dill, roughly chopped
instructions: With olive oil, cook the onion for a few minutes until soft. Add the chickpeas and season with black pepper. Stir occasionally until the chickpeas begin to crisp (a few minutes). Add the garlic, turmeric, crushed red pepper, and lemon zest. Cook until the garlic is fragrant, about 1 minute.

Add 3 cups broth and lemon juice. Bring the mix to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a simmer. Stir in the spinach, cilantro, parsley, and dill. Simmer 10-15 minutes until the spinach is wilted and the stew is very fragrant.

title: Protein bagels
ingredients: container cottage cheese (about a cup) (I used 1 percent no salt added, but 4 percent is recommended in the recipe)
instructions: Stir all dry ingredients together in a large bowl.

Add in the cottage cheese and combine with your hands. “Kneed” the dough for 5 minutes (I just kept squeezing together in my hands). Dough will be extremely sticky. Section dough into about 6 equal parts.
Wash off hands (it will be easier if clean). Roll each ball of dough into a line and then make a circle like a bagel and put onto a baking pan.
Bake for 20-22 minutes at 350 degrees. Let cool for a few minutes before enjoying.
Can try other types/ratios of flours.
https://pinchmegood.com/protein-bagels-air-fryer-recipe/

title: Pumpkin Oat Pancakes
ingredients: Tbsp melted coconut oil or butter
instructions: Add all the wet ingredients to a bowl and stir. Add all the dry ingredients to another bowl and stir. Then combine into one bowl and mix until just combined. It will be thicker than a normal pancake batter consistency.
Scoop batter in about ⅛ cup for each pancake onto a frying pan and spread the pancake out immediately (it’s too thick to spread out itself). Flip.
To make quicker: Combine all dry ingredients into a bowl and then scoop out 1 cup at a time and combine with the wet ingredients that you put together at that time.

cups unsweetened almond milk or other low FODMAP milk

title: Raspberry Blueberry Bread

Mash bananas well. Add eggs, honey, and milk. Whisk to combine. Add all the dry ingredients and mix until just incorporated.

title: Red Lentil pancakes
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the egg and yogurt.
Add in the red lentil flour, salt, baking powder, and vanilla extract. Stir to combine.
Heat avocado oil on skillet. Drop the mixture into 4 small patties (flatten them a little bit as you put on).
Heat for about 5 minutes on first side (while pan is still heating up, until golden brown on bottom). Heat for about 2-3 minutes on other side.

title: Rice Krispie Treats
ingredients: two 10-ounce bags (566g; 11 heaping cups) mini marshmallows
instructions: Line a 9×13-inch baking pan with parchment paper. Lightly grease the parchment. I always use a very light mist of nonstick spray. Set aside.
Melt the butter over medium heat in a very large pot. Once melted, add the marshmallows. Stir the mixture until the marshmallows are completely melted.
Remove from heat, then immediately stir in the vanilla extract and salt. Finally, fold in the cereal. Make sure each piece of cereal is coated with the marshmallow mixture.

Transfer mixture to prepared pan. Using a rubber spatula (greasing it lightly helps), gently spread mixture to fit the pan. Lightly grease the back of a flat spatula and very gently press the mixture down into the pan. Do not pack it down with force, just lightly press down until it is secure in the pan.
Allow treats to set for at least 1 hour at room temperature, and up to 1 day. Cover tightly if leaving out for more than a few hours.
Lift the rice krispie treats as a whole out of the pan using the edges of the parchment paper. Cut into squares.
Cover and store leftover treats at room temperature for up to 3 days. To store, place in an airtight container, with sheets of parchment or wax paper between layers.

ingredients: ounces extra-firm tofu (can do whole packet)

instructions: Pat tofu dry and roll in a clean, absorbent towel with something heavy on top, such as a cast-iron skillet, for 15 minutes.
While tofu is draining, prepare sauce by adding dry spices to a small bowl and adding enough water to make a pourable sauce. Set aside.
Add kale, season with a bit more salt and pepper, and cover to steam for 2 minutes.
In the meantime, unwrap tofu and use a fork to crumble into bite-sized pieces.

Use a spatula to move the veggies to one side of the pan and add tofu. Sauté for 2 minutes, then add sauce, pouring it mostly over the tofu and a little over the veggies. Stir immediately, evenly distributing the sauce. Cook for another 5-7 minutes until tofu is slightly browned.

instructions: With olive oil, saute onions and carrots until soft/translucent. Remove and keep on the side.
With olive oil, cook the ground turkey, breaking up any large chunks, until cooked through.
Add back sauteed onions and carrots as well as all the spices and tomato paste. Stir to combine.
Add kidney beans, corn, and crushed/diced tomatoes. Stir to combine, then add enough water to almost but not quite cover everything. Bring to a boil then reduce and simmer for about 25 minutes.

title: Vegan African Peanut Stew

½ cup natural style peanut butter
instructions: Heat oil over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook for about 3 minutes, until softened. Add cumin, paprika and cinnamon. Cook for another minute.
Add the rest of the ingredients except for the greens. Bring the pot to a boil, stirring until peanut butter has dissolved into the liquid.
Cover the pot, reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 15-20 minutes (until sweet potatoes and cauliflower are soft). Stir in greens and let simmer for a couple minutes, until wilted.

title: Vegan breakfast burrito
ingredients: (14 ounce) package extra-firm water-packed tofu, drained and crumbled
(15 ounce) can reduced-sodium black beans, rinsed
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add tofu, chili powder, cumin and salt; cook, stirring, until the tofu is nicely browned, 10 to 12 minutes. Transfer to a bowl.

Add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil to the pan. Add beans, corn and scallions and cook, stirring, until the scallions have softened, about 3 minutes. Return the tofu to the pan. Add salsa and cilantro; cook, stirring, until heated through, about 2 minutes more.

If serving immediately, warm tortillas (or wraps), but if freezing do not warm them. Divide the bean mixture among the tortillas, spreading evenly over the bottom third of each tortilla. Roll snugly, tucking in the ends as you go. Serve immediately or wrap each burrito in foil and freeze for up to 3 months.
If frozen,  heat in the microwave: Remove foil, cover with a paper towel and microwave on High until hot, 1 1/2 to 2 minutes.
https://www.eatingwell.com/recipe/268870/vegan-freezer-breakfast-burritos/

title: Veggie broth
instructions: Add any kind of veggie scraps (I keep in the freezer until ready to make, such as onion peels, ends of zucchini, kale stems, sweet potato skins, ends of winter squash, butternut squash skins)
I don’t use soft/starchy veggies, like regular potato skins or red pepper
Water to match top of veggie scraps (based on how much broth you want to make)
Directions: Manual 10 minutes, slow release

title: Veggie Red Curry
ingredients: Thai basil for topping (if not using, use 4-5 tsp red curry paste for more kick)
instructions: Saute onions for a few minutes until soft. Add garlic and ginger and saute for one minute. Add red curry paste and saute for another minute. Then turn off instant pot and stir in coconut milk.
Stir in veggies and tofu. Set to Manual for 4 minutes. Slow release.

title: Veggie ziti bake
cookTime: pasta. once done and drained, mix in the pasta sauce, italian seasoning, black pepper, and 1 cup of the mozzarella cheese.
ingredients: Trader Joe's grilled peppers and onions bag
instructions: Put half of the pasta veggies mixture in a large baking dish. Plop half the cottage cheese on top in dabs throughout. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese on top.
Repeat again with the rest of the pasta veggie mixture and then rest of the cottage cheese and then mozzarella cheese on top.

title: Vish's Mom's Moong Dal
ingredients: Parsley, optional (preferred if no curry leaves)
Veggies (fresh or frozen) (options: potatoes (cut large), carrots, green beans, bell pepper, broccoli, spinach)
instructions: Boil moong dal and water until moong dal is split and easy to blend (start with about 3 cups of water for 1 cup moong dal and then keep adding water when getting low).
Blend moong dal until smooth.
Heat oil, add cumin and mustard seed and fry.
Add back moong dal and more water (to get preferred consistency) -> maybe 1 or 2 cups more water.
Bring to a boil and then add some turmeric for color, curry leaves (if using), a pinch of salt, and grated tomatoes.
Bring again to a boil and then add all veggies, except anything soft like spinach.
Once veggies are soft (especially careful to not over boil potatoes), stir in lemon juice, parsley, diced green chilis, and spinach (if using).

title: What is Low FODMAP
instructions: You may have heard of the FODMAP diet from a friend or on the internet. When people say “FODMAP diet,” they usually mean a diet low in FODMAP — certain sugars that may cause intestinal distress. This diet is designed to help people with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and/or small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) figure out which foods are problematic and which foods reduce symptoms.

“The low FODMAP diet is a temporary eating plan that’s very restrictive,” says Johns Hopkins gastroenterologist Hazel Galon Veloso, M.D. “It’s always good to talk to your doctor before starting a new diet, but especially with the low FODMAP diet since it eliminates so many foods — it’s not a diet anyone should follow for long. It’s a short discovery process to determine what foods are troublesome for you.”

FODMAP stands for fermentable oligosaccharides, disaccharides, monosaccharides and polyols, which are short-chain carbohydrates (sugars) that the small intestine absorbs poorly. Some people experience digestive distress after eating them.
Low FODMAP three step elimination diet:
Low FODMAP is a three-step elimination diet:
First, you stop eating certain foods (high FODMAP foods).
Next, you slowly reintroduce them to see which ones are troublesome.
Once you identify the foods that cause symptoms, you can avoid or limit them while enjoying everything else worry-free.

“We recommend following the elimination portion of the diet for only two to six weeks,” says Veloso. “This reduces your symptoms and if you have SIBO, it can help decrease abnormally high levels of intestinal bacteria. Then every three days, you can add a high FODMAP food back into your diet, one at a time, to see if it causes any symptoms. If a particular high FODMAP food causes symptoms, then avoid this long term.”
High FODMAP foods to avoid:
Dairy-based milk, yogurt and ice cream
Wheat-based products such as cereal, bread and crackers
Beans and lentils
Some vegetables, such as artichokes, asparagus, onions and garlic
Some fruits, such as apples, cherries, pears and peaches
Low FODMAP foods to eat:
Eggs and meat
Certain cheeses such as brie, Camembert, cheddar and feta
Almond milk
Grains like rice, quinoa and oats
Vegetables like eggplant, potatoes, tomatoes, cucumbers and zucchini
Fruits such as grapes, oranges, strawberries, blueberries and pineapple

title: White Chicken Chili
ingredients: 15-oz cans cannellini white beans, drained and rinsed
cup of chicken broth or water
instructions: Add onion, beans, corn, broth, green chiles, cuming, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, pepper to the Instant Pot. Lay chicken on top.
Ensure the lid is on "sealing". Cook on high pressure for 20 minutes. It'll take about 10-15 minutes for it to get up to pressure. Release pressure after 10 minute natural release.
Take the chicken out and shred it with two forks. Return to the pot with yogurt and optional lime. Serve with cilantro.

title: Whole Wheat Bread Maker Bread
ingredients: ¾ tsp active dry yeast of 1 ¼ tsp quick yeast
instructions: Add water, olive oil, maple syrup/honey, salt to bread maker. Stir slightly.
Pour flour on top (make sure fully covering the wet ingredients (do not stir in)).
Make a line in the flour. Pour the yeast into that line.
Set bread maker to whole wheat.
Take off and cool upside down on a wire rack as soon as possible once bread maker finished.

title: Wild Salmon
instructions: Add one cup of water to the instant pot. Put trivet in instant pot. Place fresh or frozen wild salmon in about 1 layer (okay if a little overlap) on trivet.
Frozen: Manual 5 minutes, quick release
Fresh: Manual 3 minutes, quick release
Good with pesto on top (see sauces), or whisked mixture of lemon zest, lemon, olive oil, black pepper, dried thyme

title: Zucchini artichoke bites
ingredients: ½ cup canned artichoke hearts, drained and chopped
Pinch of salt, black pepper, and crushed red pepper flakes
medium zucchini, cut into ½ inch rounds
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F. Put zucchini rounds on a baking tray.
Combine all ingredients except for the zucchini in a medium size bowl.
Spread about 1 Tbsp of mixture on top of each zucchini coin.
Bake until zucchini is tender and cheese is melty, about 15 minutes.

title: Zucchini bread smoothie
ingredients: /4 cup frozen zucchini (mine is cut in quarters)
/2 cup non-dairy milk (code RACHL for 15% off)
/4 cup yogurt of choice (dairy or non-dairy works)
Dash of cinnamon if you’d like
instructions: Blend all together
Add more milk if necessary

title: Zucchini Chickpea Tomato Quinoa
cookTime: zucchini strips in a large pan until mostly soft. add chickpeas now or earlier while zucchini is cooking depending on how soft you like them.
ingredients: About 8 oz small tomatoes (such as grape tomatoes), cut in half
instructions: Add garlic and ground pepper and mix until combined (stove is still on).
Add tomatoes and cook a few minutes longer.
Turn off the stove and mix in the cooked quinoa.
Parmesan cheese may be added on top when serving.

title: Zucchini turkey burgers
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F
Combine all ingredients in a large bowl and combine. Do not over mix.
Roll into balls and bake the meatballs for 20-30 minutes until they reach an internal temperature of at least 165F.
